portfolio_rationale: LLY leads with exceptional fundamental quality (42.5% revenue growth, near-50% operating margins) and a bear case that was factually inaccurate, making it the highest-conviction holding. BRK.B earns the second slot due to the cleanest technical picture in the universe combined with solid fundamentals and a weak bear case. AAPL and GOOGL receive smaller allocations as secondary bullish ideas with meaningful technical headwinds, while XOM is excluded due to overbought conditions and thin fundamental support, and AMZN is excluded due to extreme technical damage (-16.5% in 20 days) despite strong fundamentals.

LLY.reason: Best fundamental quality in the universe with 42.5% revenue growth and near-50% operating margins; bear case was factually wrong, giving highest conviction.

BRK.B.reason: Cleanest technical setup (price above 20-day MA, MACD positive, positive 20-day momentum) combined with strong profitability metrics and a weak bear case.

AAPL.reason: Consistent three-week bullish signal history with strong margins and revenue growth, though near-term technical headwinds limit sizing.

GOOGL.reason: Strong fundamentals and deeply oversold RSI (23) suggest rebound potential, but recent bearish judge signal and persistent negative momentum reduce conviction.

XOM.reason: Overbought RSI (70+), thin fundamental data, litigious news risk, and quick liquidity concern make the risk/reward unattractive relative to peers.

AMZN.reason: Extreme technical damage (-16.5% in 20 days, RSI at 16.7) signals continued selling pressure that outweighs strong fundamental metrics.
